recipes.rootCause:
resend's logo-wall
(12 logo imgs, txtLen 75 < 120) tripped the media>=4 mockup rasterizer, baking its native intro line into pixels + dropping it. Text was also missed by hasOwnText because resend wraps it

text

If none found, unchanged.

recipes.rootCause: Round-1's div-code-editor detector (descendant-mono) was TOO BROAD — fired on any container merely CONTAINING a <code>/<pre> or whose first span was mono. tailwind prose sections cite inline class names (text-8xl) + have a mono first-span, so whole prose cards collapsed into one 'code blob' → ~93% of tailwind text lost (coverage 0.06).
recipes.diffSummary: Replaced the over-broad mono flag with a mono-DOMINANCE gate: new monoTextFrac(e) helper walks text nodes returning the fraction of text rendered monospace; treat as code editor iff isMono(el) || monoTextFrac(el) >= 0.6. Genuine code editor ~1.0 mono vs prose-citing-code ~0.39 → clean separation. tailwind coverage 0.06→0.24, 'why tailwind css?' recovered as editable heading; resend's real code editors still capture as code.
recipes.generalizes: Any prose-heavy page that cites inline code — no longer eaten by the code-editor detector.

recipes.rootCause: FIRST structural-fidelity round under the element-aware grader. The corpus has 31 real <ul>/<ol> lists but build-absolute rebuilt ZERO native list widgets — capture's generic walk() recursed each list into per-<li> text leaves (or flattened/dropped them), so structuralFidelity for 'list' was 0 everywhere lists exist (0.3 composite weight → big drag).
recipes.diffSummary: TWO purely-additive edits per ELEMENTOR_CAPABILITY_MATRIX 'list' row (mechanism: text-editor <ul>/<ol>, native ~100%; icon-list AVOIDED — matrix flags icon controls flaky on this stack). (a) capture-layout.mjs walk(): added a 'list' node-kind detector (after accordion, before generic recursion) matching the grader's exact gate (<ul>/<ol> with >=3 DIRECT visible <li>, NOT in nav) → emits ONE {kind:'list',tag,ordered,box,typo,items[{text,href}]} node, keeping the ordered flag + per-item href for single-link items. (b) build-absolute.mjs leafWidget(): n.kind==='list' branch emits a native text-editor widget whose editor HTML is a real <ul>/<ol><li>… (single-link items kept as <a href>) — kses-safe, renders a true list in the live DOM (exactly what grade-sections counts). Routing automatic (flatten()→leafWidget()).
recipes.generalizes: Every site with real bullet/numbered lists (feature lists, footers, pricing rows) now rebuilds them as native, editable, correctly-typed Elementor lists instead of loose text — the first compounding structural recipe.

(1) Append a scoped @media(max-width:1024px) un-pin of .elementor-absolute (position:relative + left/top/right/bottom:auto + width/max-width:100% + margin:0 0 12px 0) to page_settings.custom_css — the SAME Elementor-Pro custom_css channel build-absolute already uses for @font-face (proven kses-safe + round-trips, 268-char string intact). recipes.criticalFinding: Cracks absolute's ONLY weakness (it was desktop-pixel-frozen, the reason flow existed). KEY MECHANISM: the kses-safe responsive path is a page custom_css media query, NOT per-breakpoint position keys (CORRECTED assumption: _tablet/_mobile keys are NOT kses-stripped, but _position is simply not a RESPONSIVE Elementor control so _position_mobile is stored-but-inert; .elementor-absolute welds position:absolute at all widths -> only a custom_css override un-pins it). Desktop stays 1:1 (scoped <=1024); mobile/tablet reflow to single column (docW==viewport, no h-scroll).
